title: if `peer_relations` is `None`, attempting to access `.units` will raise an `AttributeError`
summary: In lib/charms/prometheus_k8s/v1/prometheus_remote_write.py, _push_alerts_to_relation_databag calls model.get_relation() which may return None, then guards with a truthiness check (`if peer_relations`) rather than an explicit `is not None` check before accessing peer_relations.units. The issue (raised from a PR review) asks to switch to explicit None checking.
why_good_first_issue: Single-line, well-scoped change with the exact fix already proposed in the issue body; touches one library function; no architectural knowledge needed; an existing unit test file (test_absent_alert_duplicated_for_remote_writer.py) already exercises the peer-relation path so the change is easy to validate.
repro_steps: On VM cos: git clone --depth 80 the repo into ~/work; grep -n 'peer_relations' lib/charms/prometheus_k8s/v1/prometheus_remote_write.py; sed -n '506,509p' the file to read the exact code.
repro_result: Confirmed at lib/charms/prometheus_k8s/v1/prometheus_remote_write.py:506-509 (HEAD 14e9a3a, LIBAPI=1 LIBPATCH=14). Observed: `peer_relations = self._charm.model.get_relation(self._peer_relation_name)` followed by `{unit.name for unit in peer_relations.units} if peer_relations else set()`. Expected per issue: `... if peer_relations is not None else set()`. The truthiness guard is the only None-style guard of its kind in the file (other guard at line 482 uses explicit `is not None`), confirming the inconsistency.
fix_hint: Replace the `if peer_relations` truthiness check on line 508 with `if peer_relations is not None`. Bump LIBPATCH in the same file (charm-library convention). Optionally extend test_absent_alert_duplicated_for_remote_writer.py with a case where the peer relation is absent (get_relation returns None).
notes: Labeled Type: Enhancement upstream but is really a defensive-coding/cleanup fix. Note: ops Relation objects are normally truthy, so the practical behaviour change is minimal; the value is clarity/correctness against the documented get_relation() -> Optional[Relation] contract. Verified not yet fixed on HEAD.

title: prometheus charm instantiates GrafanaSourceProvider with `external_url`, then reconfigures it to internal_url
suggested_labels: good first issue, Type: Bug, Priority: Low
summary: GrafanaSourceProvider is constructed at import/init time with source_url=self.most_external_url (an external URL when ingress is set), but during _configure() it is updated via update_source(self.internal_url). Because the provider is instantiated on every event while update_source only runs on a subset of events, there can be windows where the externally-advertised source URL is published to Grafana instead of the intended internal URL.
why_good_first_issue: Small, self-contained inconsistency in a single file (src/charm.py); the fix is to make the init value and the update value consistent (or document why they differ); Priority: Low and already triaged; no deep architecture needed beyond reading two nearby code paths.
repro_steps: On VM cos: clone repo into ~/work; grep -n 'GrafanaSourceProvider|most_external_url|internal_url|update_source' src/charm.py; sed -n '251,256p' and sed -n '690,696p' src/charm.py to read both code paths.
repro_result: Confirmed at HEAD 14e9a3a. Observed mismatch: src/charm.py:254 `source_url=self.most_external_url` at GrafanaSourceProvider instantiation, vs src/charm.py:693 `self.grafana_source_provider.update_source(self.internal_url)` inside _configure(). most_external_url returns external_url when ingress is configured (src/charm.py:503-514), so init publishes external while reconfigure publishes internal. A comment at src/charm.py:691-692 explains internal_url is intentional (refs operator issue #970), confirming internal is the desired value and the init value is the inconsistent one.
fix_hint: Change the GrafanaSourceProvider instantiation on src/charm.py:254 to source_url=self.internal_url for consistency with update_source (and the comment at lines 691-692), or add a brief comment explaining the init-vs-update difference if external is intentional at init. Add/adjust a unit test asserting the grafana-source databag carries internal_url.
notes: Issue body is somewhat speculative ('I don't know if this is a bug with a consequence'); good first issue partly because investigating and either fixing or documenting is itself the deliverable. Verified the mismatch still present on HEAD.
